characters.name: Aaron Martin
characters.description: Aaron MartinDominic's best friend from his youthJason Bernard as Aaron Martin inSins of the Past.Credit InfoFirst appearanceSins of The Past (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byJason BernardInformationNickname(s)AliasesGendermaleOccupation???TitleStatusKilled by Jason DariusNationalitySan RemoAaron Martinis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 2 episode titled"Sins of The Past". Aaron Martin is Dominic's best friend from the days of his youth on the island of San Remo in the Caribbean. He notified Dominic of the death of his daughter Sally Ann Santini whom he had not seen for many years ever since he was estranged from his wifeLila Santini. At Sally Ann's funeral, Aaron told Dominic that Sally Ann still loved him very much in spite of her mother Lila's hostility toward him. When Lila was murdered and Dominic was arrested for the crime, Hawke returned to San Remo to clear his name. He met Aaron again. Aaron fills Hawke in on Dominic's past and Lila's dark history. together they go to a house which Dominic had kept all the years in case Sally Ann would come back. Someone tries to break into it. Later, they find the house ransacked. Who are these men and what do they want?Vince Dectertells Hawke of something strange Lila had said: that if anyone came after her, her baby would protect her. Aaron has an idea and wants to meet Hawke at the house. Hawke rushes there but finds Aaron killed by some toughs sent out byJason Darius. What Aaron had suspected was that Lila kept something in the house which incriminated Darius in something. She had been using that as protection against him.

characters.name: Alvin Brock
characters.description: Alvin BrockDrug pusher/distributorAlvin BrockWildfire (episode)Credit InfoFirst appearanceWildfire (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byTracy WalterInformationNickname(s)AliasesGendermaleOccupationDrug pusherTitleStatusArrestedNationalityAmerican Alvin Brockis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 3 episode titled"Wildfire". Alvin Brock is one of the drug pushers working withCarlos,Noble FlowersandMooseto bring in drugs usingBig Cece'soil refinery as a cover. WhenLittle Cecewas convinced by Dominic to refuse to deliver a shipment and thus expose his operation, he and Carlos went after the Cece family. They caught up with them in a hotel. Alvin demanded that Big Cece hand over his package of drugs but Big Cece and Dominic overpowered him and Carlos in a coordinated attack which they had developed during a bar fight when they were servicemen in Korea. Alvin and Carlos were knocked out and it is likely that Dominic and Big Cece would have tied them up for the authorities to pick up later.

characters.name: Amanda
characters.description: AmandaAide to ArchangelKandace Kuehl as Amanda, another of Archangel's aides to appear on the show.Credit InfoFirst appearanceAirwolf II (episode)Last appearancePortrayed byKandace KuehlInformationNickname(s)AliasesGenderFemaleOccupationF.I.R.M. employeeTitleAideStatus?NationalityAmericanAmandais the name of an aide to Archangel who makes a single appearance in the Season 3 episodeAirwolf II (episode). Amanda is the first among Archangel's aides to appear in Season 3. LikeMarellaandLauraandMeryl, she is capable, quick with her facts and also a helicopter pilot. At the beginning of the episode, she flies Archangel to meet Hawke to ask for an explanation as to why a Santini Air chopper was seen to be involved in a raid/robbery on an armaments train. Later she drives Archangel to a F.I.R.M. Committee meeting. There they meetHarlan "Tex" Jenkinswho tries to chat her up but she doesn't say anything. Archangel tells Jenkin she only talks when it pleases her, or if he tells her to. He also warns him not to make a move on her as she is "a cobra".

characters.name: Anastasia Zarcov
characters.description: Anastasia ZarcovRussian agent who poses as Dr. Lisa Holgate, psychiatristZohra Lampert as Anastasia Zarcov in Echoes From The PastCredit InfoFirst appearanceEchoes From The Past (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byZohra LampertInformationNickname(s)AliasesGenderfemaleOccupationintelligence agentTitleposes as a psychiatristStatusunknownNationalityRussian Anastasia Zarcovis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 1 episode titled"Echoes From The Past". Anastasia Zarcov is a Russian agent who specialises in researching and using behavior-modifying drugs. After Hawke been drugged and brought to a hospital, she poses as a psychiatrist Dr. Lisa Holgate, with the job of helping Hawke readjust to a normal life after (so he is told) being in a coma for a year. She applies medications to Hawke to keep him groggy and maintain the deception, especially that a fakeSaint John Hawkethey have brought in is the real thing. The leader of the intelligence gang, East GermanEric Massis impatient and wants to question Hawke about the location of Airwolf and wants her to reduce his medication. Zarcov tells him it is too soon and that their methods are not foolproof but Mass insists. This turns out to be the gang's undoing. As she predicts, Hawke soon sees through the whole thing and tries to escape.

characters.name: Angela
characters.description: AngelaPassing motorist who rescues HawkeRachel Brady as Angela inHX-1 (episode).Credit InfoFirst appearanceHX-1 (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byRachel BradyInformationNickname(s)AliasesGenderfemaleOccupationnot statedTitleStatusNationalityAmerican Angelais a character who makes a brief one-time appearance in the Season 2 episode titled"HX-1". Hawke has been knocked out byDunkirk'sgang and dumped in the desert to die. When he comes to, he spots a car approaching driven by Angela. Angela stops for him and Hawke walks over but he collapses from thirst. Angela revives him with some water and wants to take him to a hospital but he asks to be taken to the nearest payphone where he calls for Dominic to collect him in Airwolf.

characters.name: Angelo
characters.description: AngeloRuns a aircraft paint shop at Van NuysJohn O'Connell as Angelo inSantini's Millions (episode).Credit InfoFirst appearanceSantini's Millions (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byJohn O'ConnellInformationNickname(s)AliasesGendermaleOccupationpainterTitleStatusNationalityAmerican Angelois a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 2 episode titled"Santin's Million". Angelo runs the paint shop across the runway from Santini Air at Van Nuys. Earlier the in the episode, a Jetranger in Santini Air colors picksMargo Brewsterup and she disappears.James GraydonandCooperturn up at Santini Air to demand to know what happened to her. Dominic insists that his Jetranger is not serviceable and shows them the damaged tail rotor but Graydon insists that it was definitely a red, white and blue painted chopper exactly like Dominic's. Caitlin suggests that they go see Angelo as he does the paint jobs for Santini Air's choppers.

characters.name: Angie
characters.description: AngieDominic's friend, runs a bar at "Four Corners"Marilyn Schreffler as Angie inFirestorm (episode).Credit InfoFirst appearanceFirestorm (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byMarilyn SchrefflerInformationNickname(s)AliasesGenderfemaleOccupationbar ownerTitle-Status-NationalityAmerican Angieis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 2 episode titled"Firestorm". Angie is a friend of Dominic's and ran a bar somewhere in the desert. WhenEddie Donahoughhad too much to drink and was getting violent, she asks Dominic (who brings Hawke along) to help take Eddie home.

characters.name: Annie
characters.description: AnnieOwner of a shop selling rocks and fossilsEve McVeagh as the Annie, the rock collector who sees too much.Credit InfoFirst appearanceRandom Target (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byEve McVeaghInformationNickname(s)AliasesGenderFemaleOccupationRock collector, shop ownerTitle-StatusKilled by Langhorn's helicopter gunshipsNationalityAmerican Annieis a rock collector who makes a one time appearance in the Season 2 episode titled"Random Target". Annie is described as "a harmless old lady" who "lives in a world by herself". She owns "Annie's Rock Shop" in Silver Springs (probably Silver Springs, Nevada, and makes trips into the desert to collect rock samples for sale in her shop.

characters.name: Armand
characters.description: ArmandArms Buyer, potential buyer of stolen HX-1Armand (right) withDunkirkinHX-1 (episode)Credit InfoFirst appearanceHX-1 (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byRené AssaInformationNickname(s)AliasesGendermaleOccupationgovernment officialTitleStatuskilled when HX-1 crashed on Dunkirk's arms display at Red Rock FlatsNationalityLibyan? Armandis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 2 episode titled"HX-1". In the episode,Dunkirk, the leader of a mercenary gang has stolen the HX-1 helicopter and is planning to sell it along with other weapons to buyers representing various foreign governments. These buyers have all been invited to an arms display at Red Rock Flats. Armand is a potential buyer for the HX-1 helicopter. Armand's nationality is not given explicitly but there is a suggestion that he is Libyan or a competitor to buyers from Libya. For example, earlier in the episode, Dunkirk had complained to his gang that Armand had been holding up the display and causing delays. He adds that may be they just have cut the HX-1 up into pieces and ship it to Libya.

characters.name: Arnie
characters.description: ArniePilot, friend of Herb WaldrenJack Hogan as Peters.Credit InfoFirst appearanceLittle Wolf (episode)Last appearancen/a single appearancePortrayed byGrainger HinesInformationNickname(s)AliasesGendermaleOccupationPilotTitle-StatusActiveNationalityAmericanArnieis the name of a character who makes a single appearance in the Season 3 episodeLittle Wolf (episode). Arnie is a pilot and flies a Jetranger. He is also a friend ofHerb Waldren. Arnie helped Waldren fly the dead body ofGreg Stewartto the Stewart house where it was hidden in the cellar. Later, when Hawke escaped from the Stewart house, Waldren called on Arnie to join the pursuit. Arnie was at that time flying his helicopter equipped for crop dusting. He sprayed the area with pesticide, hoping to impede Hawke but this also had the effect of reducing ground visibility and making it difficult for the pursuers. Arnie sprays pesticide on Hawke from his crop dusting chopper.

characters.name: Barkley Kase
characters.description: Barkley KaseNational Banking Adviser, Member of Cullen Dixon conspiracyBarkley Kase at Tess Dixon's hearing inDay of Jeopardy (episode)Credit InfoFirst appearanceDay of Jeopardy (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byG. Gordon LiddyInformationNickname(s)AliasesGendermaleOccupationGovernment officialTitleStatusCommitted suicideNationalityAmerican Barkley Kaseis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 3 episode titled"Day of Jeopardy". Kase was a senior official in the U.S. government and held the position of National Banking Adviser. He was also a member of Cullen Dixon's conspiracy to assassinate world leaders and take control of world currency. He acted in the role of the "chief operations officer" of the conspiracy and executed plans which the conspiracy. WhenTess Dixonescaped, he was forced to organise plans to prevent her from reaching a Senate committee to testify against the conspiracy. Kase had vast resources at his disposal including helicopter gunships, F-16 fighters, ground forces as well as a last resort plan code named "Blue Star" in the form of a traitor within the FIRM. In his position, Kase probably had direct access to the President and was either able to issue orders on his behalf or persuade the President to order a particular course of events. For example, Archangel told Hawke that he had direct orders from the White House to force Airwolf to follow an automatic computer controlled flight plan via ground control, even though everyone among the crew thought it was a bad idea. This ground control almost led Airwolf to come to grief after being intercepted by some of Kase's F-16s until Dominic could disable it.

characters.name: Beatrice Maretti
characters.description: Beatrice MarettiFashion designer on San Remo, best friend of Lila SantiniLola Albright as Beatrice Maretti inSins of the Past.Credit InfoFirst appearanceSins of The Past (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byLola AlbrightInformationNickname(s)AliasesGenderfemaleOccupationFashion designerTitleStatus???NationalitySan RemoBeatrice Marettiis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 2 episode titled"Sins of The Past". Beatrice Maretti is a fashion designer on the island of San Remo. She was also the best friend of Dominic's estranged wifeLila Santini. According toAaron Martin, if Lila ever confided in anyone, it would be Beatrice.  When Lila was murdered and Dominic was arrested for the crime, Hawke returned to the island to clear his name. He met Beatrice at a fashion show she was staging for her new "Fall line". Beatrice told him that Lila was good at handling and using men. Dominic, for example, was used. Lila told him she was pregnant and they got married, but she wasn't.  As Hawke concluded from her talk, it sounded like Lila used and therefore hurt a lot of men. Beatrice Maretti is played byLola Albright[1]This is her only appearance on Airwolf.

characters.name: Bingham
characters.description: BinghamMember of F.I.R.M. CommitteeBingham, in Zeus' office. She is trying to help Zeus persuade Archangel to help the F.I.R.M.Credit InfoFirst appearanceAirwolf II (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearancePortrayed byLillian LehmanInformationNickname(s)AliasesGenderFemaleOccupationFederal Civil ServantTitleStatusNationalityAmericanBinghamis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 3 episode titled"Airwolf II". Bingham (her first name is unknown) is a member of theF.I.R.M.Committee. She is present when the Committee confronts Archangel andZeusfires him because they now have Redwolf. However, Redwolf's test pilotHarlan "Tex" Jenkinssteals his own helicopter. Zeus has no choice but to turn to Archangel again. Bingham and a few other Committee members are there with Zeus to help persuade Archangel to use Airwolf to either retrieve Redwolf or destroy it. While Bingham's position in the F.I.R.M. is not specified, her presence with Zeus at this second meeting suggests that she is one of the more senior members of the committee. Bingham is played byLillian Lehman.[1]

characters.name: Blaze
characters.description: BlazeMovie StarJohn Calvin inShadow of The Hawke (episode)Credit InfoFirst appearanceShadow of The Hawke (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byJohn CalvinInformationNickname(s)AliasesGendermaleOccupationTitleStatusStill acting, suitably chastened?NationalityAmerican Blazeis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Pilot/Season 1 episode titled"Shadow of The Hawke. Blaze is an actor who plays a police helicopter pilot in a movie being directed byMitchfor which Dominic and Hawke are providing the helicopter stunt flying. Blaze has heard that reporterMona Kahnis coming to film him at work and wants to do his own stunt flying to impress her. He claims that he has flown a hang glider and a navy jet in previous movies. What's more, Hawke had been allowing him to fly the helicopter for the past three months of filming (although Hawke has been backing him up by correcting his mistakes).

characters.name: Brother Ahab
characters.description: Brother AhabTerroristGregory Walcott as Brother Ahab inDambreakers (episode).Credit InfoFirst appearanceDambreakers (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byGregory WalcottInformationNickname(s)AliasesBrother Ahab is his cover name. Real name unknown.GendermaleOccupationTitleStatusnot knownNationalityunknown Brother Ahabis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 2 episode titled"Dambreakers". Brother Ahab is the cover name used by one of the terrorists led byJohann Rector. His group had taken a "back to nature" religious community hostage and were using it as a cover to mount an operation to blow a hydroelectric dam.

characters.name: Dr. Roger Burton
characters.description: Dr. Roger BurtonScientist, passenger for Airwolf's special deliveryDr. Roger Burton (Donald Hotton) evades Russian helicopter patrols while waiting for Airwolf.Credit InfoFirst appearanceMoffett's Ghost (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byDonald HottonInformationNickname(s)AliasesGenderMaleOccupationScientistTitle-StatusSuccessfully extracted from his secret meeting in SiberiaNationalityAmerican Dr.Roger Burtonis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 2 episode titled""Moffett's Ghost". Dr. Roger Burton is an American scientist who meets secretly with a group of Russian scientists once a year in Siberia to exchange information. These meetings have, according to him, helped to cool down the arms race. Infiltrating him into Siberia and then exfiltrating him after the meeting is the F.I.R.M.'s responsibility and Archangel has asked Hawke and Dominic to undertake the mission in Airwolf. Hawke enjoys the irony, because sneaking past Russian air defenses is normal but he has to sneak past American air defenses for this flight as well. Nonetheless, it's a mission everyone feels good about--as Marella says, if his work didn't have to be conducted in secret, he would have won a fistful of Nobel Peaces Prizes. Dominic and Hawke share the sentiment, which is why they are determined, after successfully inserting him into Siberia, to return a few days later to extract him even though Airwolf's computer is not in good health.

characters.description: Texas Highway Patrol chopper pilot appeared inSeason TwoofAirwolfin the episodeSweet Britchesand also inseason 3. Often described as feisty, red heading and in her twenties.[1]She more than earned her spot on theAirwolfteam.  Her story about joining the Team.The Truth About Holly[2]

characters.name: Captain Nuzo
characters.description: Captain NuzoPilot of U.S.A.F. KC-135 tankerChris Capen as Captain Nuzo in Proof Through the NightCredit InfoFirst appearanceProof Through the Night (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byChris CapenInformationNickname(s)AliasesGendermaleOccupationpilotTitleCaptainStatusDid he win the $10 the second time?NationalityAmerican Captain Nuzois a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 1 episode titledProof Through the Night. Captain Nuzo is the pilot of a U.S.A.F. KC-135 tanker tasked to refuel Airwolf during its mission to rescueVladimir Rostofffrom Russia. Out over the Baltic Sea, he makes a $10 bet with his co-pilot about the type of secret aircraft they are refueling. Nuzo is sure it is an SR-71. His co-pilot says it is a Harrier. When Airwolf arrives to hook up, Nuzo asks the boom operatorSergeant Tembruscowhat type of aircraft it is. Tembrusco is so stunned to see Airwolf he tells Nuzo, "I'd rather not say...."

characters.name: Carl Zimmer
characters.description: Carl ZimmerKGB AgentCharles Robinson inDaddy's Gone A Hunt'n (episode)Credit InfoFirst appearanceDaddy's Gone A Hunt'n (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byCharles RobinsonInformationNickname(s)AliasesGendermaleOccupationIntelligence agentTitle-StatusProbably punished for his failureNationalityRussian Carl Zimmeris a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 1 episode titledDaddy's Gone A Hunt'n (episode). Carl Zimmer is a KGB intelligence agent. He scours the streets of Ho Chi Minh city and findsHo Minh Truong, the son of USAFMajor Sam Roperand his Vietnamese wifeNhi Houng Roper. He then contacts the Ropers and offers to trade the boy for the latest American tactical fighter. Major Roper must divert the aircraft during a ferry flight to Alaska and take the plane to Omrylkot in Russia. The plan fails as the FIRM gets intelligence about it and sends Hawke to intervene. Roper is stopped from delivering the plane. Instead, Airwolf flies to Omrylkot and rescues the boy. Zimmer is left alive at Omrylkot after Airwolf's raid. It is not clear what happened to him but it is likely that he is punished for his failure.

characters.name: Carlos
characters.description: CarlosDrug pusher/distributorCarlos inWildfire (episode)Credit InfoFirst appearanceWildfire (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byIsmael "East" CarloInformationNickname(s)AliasesGendermaleOccupationDrug pusherTitleStatusArrestedNationalityAmerican Carlosis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 3 episode titled"Wildfire". Carlos is one of the drug pushers working withAlvin Brock,Noble FlowersandMooseto bring in drugs usingBig Cece'soil refinery as a cover. WhenLittle Cecewas convinced by Dominic to refuse to deliver a shipment and thus expose his operation, Carlos  and Alvin went after the Cece family. They caught up with them in a hotel but were overpowered by Dominic and Big Cece in a coordinated attack which they had developed during a bar fight when they were servicemen in Korea. Carlos and Alvin were knocked out and it is likely that Dominic and Big Cece would have tied them up for the authorities to pick up later.

characters.name: Carlson
characters.description: CarlsonFilm director at Caitlin's first stunt flying gigCarlson (David Ruprecht) tells the Santini Air people he really needs the stunt done in one take.Credit InfoFirst appearanceThe Truth About Holly (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byDavid RuprechtInformationNickname(s)AliasesGenderMaleOccupationFilm DirectorTitle-StatusDid he finally use the footage of Caitlin's flight?NationalityAmerican Carlsonis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 2 episode titledThe Truth About Holly (episode). David Ruprecht's character is not named in the credits but Hawke addresses him during the episode as "Mr. Carlson". Carlson is directing a film in which Santini Air is providing the helicopter stunt flying. He tells them he has problems. He is over budget and over schedule so he tells them he really needs them to complete the stunt flying scene in one take. Dominic has just hired Caitlin and this will be her first gig. It involves her playing a pilot who flies in to rescue her war correspondent boyfriend when she gets hit by gunfire, spins around wildly a bit and then land behind a building with a special effects explosion.

characters.name: Carter Anderson III
characters.description: Carter Anderson IIIIndustrialistJoseph Chapman as Carter Anderson III inThe Hunted (episode)Credit InfoFirst appearanceThe Hunted (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byJoseph ChapmanInformationNickname(s)AliasesGenderMaleOccupationIndustrialistTitleStatusSurvived attempt to shoot his aircraft downNationalityAmericanCarter Anderson IIIis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 2 episode titled"The Hunted". Carter Anserson III is an extremely wealthy and powerful industrialist. His aide describes him as  "Industrialist, philanthropist, patron of the arts, advisor to the President on military technology." In the episode, he planned to invest hundreds of millions of dollars to rehabilitate the factories of 27 failing corporations in three states. This would save fifty-thousand jobs, while making for himself "a bundle". He needed to travel to a private ceremony at a secret location to sign the deal with government, labour and corporate officials. However, there were people trying to prevent him from getting there, whom he described as vultures feeding off the failure of those corporations. According to his aide,Rosalind Gregory, there had already been three failed attempts at killing him. Through his high connections he knows Archangel and knows about Airwolf and therefore employed Santini Air to transport him to the ceremony. Hawke is impressed by his intentions (he also had Anderson's background checked, presumably with the FIRM). He agrees to transport Carter Anderson but on his terms: "You do it by my plan, my way. I'm the only one that knows. And I decide when, where and if any special equipment is to be used." Anderson's aide Rosalind Gregory thinks this is ridiculous but Anderson is shrewd enough to recognise the need for ultra security and accepts Hawke's terms.

characters.name: Charles Henry Moffett
characters.description: Credit InfoFirst appearanceShadow of the HawkeLast appearanceMoffett's Ghost(as a recording)Portrayed byDavid HemmingsInformationGenderMaleNationalityUnknown DoctorCharles Henry Moffettwas the scientist who developedAirwolf Moffet is a British scientist working for the American government He is a genius, but also a sociopath. There are indications in the pilot episode that he has perhaps had run-ins with the American government in the past, but his abilities make him too enticing for the government to pass up. Working for a division of the CIA known as the F.I.R.M., Moffet then designs and builds the prototype supersonic attack helicopter known as Airwolf, which carries a price tag of a billion dollars. During Airwolf's development, the project's chief test pilot is Vietnam veteranStringfellow Hawke, the dark and Byronic star of the series. (Hawke and Moffet never meet on screen until their final showdown; although they must have known each other; any relationship they may have had during Airwolf's development is hidden within the back-story.)

characters.name: Charles Selkirk
characters.description: Charles SelkirkCarl Barron's attorneyHank Brandt as Barron's attorney Charles Selkirk inSantini's Millions (episode).Credit InfoFirst appearanceSantini's Millions (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearance.Portrayed byHank BrandtInformationNickname(s)AliasesGendermaleOccupationattorneyTitleStatusNationalityAmerican Charles Selkirkis a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 2 episode titled"Santin's Million". Charles Selkirk is the wealthy technologist Carl Barron's attorney. After Barron's presumed death, he is present at the funeral to read out his will which leaves 40% of his estate to Dominic. Later Selkirk turns up at Santini Air to tell Dominic that the challenge to the will launched by Barron's assistantJames Graydonhad been dismissed by the court.

characters.name: Charlie Tarwater
characters.description: Charlie TarwaterLives in a cabin in Halonan National ForestRobert Wightman plays Charlie Tarwater. He and his wife have to call in Jason "Doc" Gifford for help when their baby is expected early.Credit InfoFirst appearanceInn at the End of the Road (episode)Last appearancen/a. Single appearancePortrayed byRobert WightmanInformationNickname(s)AliasesGenderMaleOccupationhomesteaderTitleStatusHis baby was delivered safely and he probably never found out that "Doc" Gifford was doing it for the first time.NationalityAmericanCharlie Tarwateris a character who makes a one-time appearance in the Season 2 episode titled"Inn at the End of the Road". Charlie Tarwater and his wifeMary Annare homesteaders who live in a small cabin somewhere in the Halonan National Forest. They had come to live off the land and be self-sufficient. However when a bridge washed away and the baby Mary Ann was carry arrived two weeks early, he had to call inJason "Doc" Giffordfor help. Charlie is more nervous than his wife about the birth and "Doc" has to calm him down by offering him drinks. But like his wife, Charlie thinks "Doc" has delivered many babies in his time and are confident in his abilities. "Doc" doesn't tell them what he tells his girlfriendMaxinelater: that he is doing it for the first time! Charlie Tarwater is played byRobert Wightman.[1]
